title: ipomoea asterophora var. subglabra
intro: Ipomoea asterophora var. subglabra is a species of morning glory plant native to Western Australia, characterized by its delicate white or pale blue flowers and slender stems. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, due to its expectorant properties. The health benefits of Ipomoea asterophora var. subglabra include its ability to relieve congestion, reduce inflammation, and promote relaxation. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water, creating a soothing tea that can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
